page: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a KVM-hypervised GPU-accelerated server designed for intensive computational workloads. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8570 processor with 48 physical cores and 48 vCPUs, operating at a base frequency of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 480.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 10.0 GB per core. For hardware acceleration, the server integrates two NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with a combined total of 384 GB of VRAM. No local storage is bundled with this instance type, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a PassMark CPU score of 88,533.65 and a stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 97,240.84, both placing in the top tier and strong categories respectively. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only large block bandwidth reaching 385.14 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 134.73 GB/sec. In cryptographic and compression tasks, the system demonstrated high throughput, recording a bzip3 multi-threaded decompression speed of 706.88 GB/sec and a PassMark CPU encryption test score of 71,927.15. For AI workloads, the server processed 135M model prompts at 9,458.91 tokens per second.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers high qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring massive parallel processing and substantial GPU memory, though the shared CPU allocation represents a potential tradeoff for sustained single-core CPU performance. Given the dual NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs and 480.0 GB of system memory, this configuration is optimized for large language model inference, deep learning training, high-performance database operations, and complex physics simulations.
description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a high-performance GPU-accelerated server utilizing KVM virtualization. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8570 processor featuring 48 physical cores, 48 vCPUs, and 480.0 GB of RAM, yielding 10.0 GB of memory per core. The hardware configuration is highlighted by two NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs providing 384 GB of total VRAM. While local storage is not included, the server delivers exceptional benchmark performance, including a PassMark CPU score of 88,533.65 and a large-block memory read bandwidth of 385.14 GB/sec. It also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in LLM prompt processing, reaching 9,458.91 tokens per second for a 135M model. This server is highly suited for deep learning, large-scale database operations, and parallel scientific computing.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-48xCPU-480GB-2xB200 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 48 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 480 GB RAM, and two NVIDIA Blackwell B200 GPUs with 384 GB VRAM for high-performance AI and database workloads.

tagline: A high-density GPU server featuring dual NVIDIA Blackwell accelerators and 480 GB RAM for demanding artificial intelligence and parallel computing workloads.
